narrative.headline: Glass and visibility issues dominate 2020 Crosstrek complaints
narrative.summary: Of 68 owner complaints filed with NHTSA, more than four in ten (44%) are about visibility and wipers, by far the biggest category. Electrical and powertrain problems are the next most common, and each was tied to one reported crash. No owner reported a fire, injury, or death in any category.

issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint area: 30 reports (44% of all complaints). No crashes, fires, or injuries were reported, but it is worth checking glass and wiper condition closely.

issues.sample: WE HAD A REMOTE START INSTALLED BY THE DEALERSHIP PRIOR TO ACCEPTING DELIVERY OF THE CAR. TWO DAYS LATER, THE CAR WOULD NOT START. THE CAR WAS COMPLETELY UNABLE TO START. THE SUBARU DEALERSHIP (SPORT SUBARU SOUTH IN ORLANDO FL) SENT A TECHNICIAN TO REPAIR THE CAR, RATHER THAN TOW
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The second most reported area: 10 reports (15%), including 1 crash. Watch for electrical faults; no fires or injuries were reported.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 9 reports (13%), including 1 crash. Several owners describe drivability problems; no fires or injuries were reported.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: A smaller cluster: 5 reports (7%). No crashes, fires, or injuries were reported.
